title: Shielded BVLOS Operations
text:
The University of California was granted a Public Safety Organization Shielded Operations Waiver for Public Agencies (91.113-2025-00194). This unique waiver authorizes beyond the visual line of sight of the remote pilot in command (which includes remote teleoperation) that are permitted in operations at or below 200 feet AGL, or within 50 feet of an object in Class G Airspace, and, at or below the UASFM altitudes in Class B, C, D or E (Surface Area) airspace whichever is lower.
This waiver is restricted to Public Aircraft Operations and may only be used by: - University of California Police Departments - University of California Fire Departments - University of California Emergency Operations Centers
These operations must support official governmental functions using University-owned aircraft and require prior approval from the UC Center of Excellence on UAS Safety.


Download the waiver here:
-Waiver



All Remote Pilots in Command intending to operate under this waiver are required to complete the following:

Register for BVLOS Operations - email UASsafety@ucmerced.edu
Part 91 Supplemental Training for Part 107 Remote Pilots
Shielded Operations Waiver Training




Flight records are required under this waiver. Please email a copy of each flight log to UASsafety@ucmerced.edu

title: UC Drones Public Safety Portal
text:
The UC Center of Excellence on Unmanned Aircraft System Safety has developed this portal of resources for all Public Safety users of Unmanned Aircraft Systems within the University of California.


For submitting UC flight requests and reports, please visit http://ehs.ucop.edu/drones






Warning



DJI, Autel, and other drones manufactured in China are banned from use on federally funded projects.
The FCC has also prohibited authorization of new foreign‑manufactured drones.
Review your fleet and projects immediately. Contact the UAS COE with any questions.





Send us an email at UASsafety@ucmerced.edu if you’d like to join our UC Drone email listserv
Want to schedule an online appointment to chat? Use our Booking link

Find a time that works for you and the system will schedule a MS Teams meeting for us.




Unmanned Aircraft System (Drone) Policy - The UC Presidential Policy on the use of Unmanned Aircraft Systems within the University of California.
Drone Map of California - Map of UC properties, County/City regulations, and public lands.
NASA ASRS - NASA UAS Safety Reporting

title: Insurance
text:
The University of California has purchased an Unmanned Aircraft System Liability Policy. This policy has a total of $5 Mil limit including Personal Injury.
Coverage is automatic for UAS activity that meet the following criteria:

Flight operations are conducted on behalf and sanctioned by the University of California.
Aircraft weight under 55 lbs (at time of takeoff)
Flight operations are within Visual Line of Sight
Flight operations are below 400 ft above ground level.
Flight operations must be conducted within the United States.

Any UAS activity that do not meet the above criteria or operate outside the above criteria must be reported to and approved by the insurance underwriter in order to be covered.


The University of California has extended their UAS liability policy to enable coverage of UAS owned by UC students, staff or faculty used for University Business, including research. Coverage is contingent on compliance with the policy and procedures on UAS usage. This coverage is not intended to cover student organizations or 3rd Party vendors or contractors.



The University of California has extended their UAS liability policy to enable coverage of UAS by Campus Police. All coverage is contingent on the UAS activity being sanctioned by the UC.
Any UAS activity that is not approved by a Designated Local Authority or Systemwide Designated UAS Authority is not covered by this liability insurance.

title: Insurance
text:
Filing a Claim
To file a claim, contact your campus Risk Manager. Please prepare the following:

Copy of the post-flight report (through UC Drones or other means of compliance)
Photographs of damaged equipment
Copy of original purchase invoice
Copy of invoice to ship the equipment back the manufacturer
Copy of repair invoice, if repairable
Copy of replacement quote, if not repairable

Ensure your coverage

Attach a copy of your UAS invoice to your UAS registration in UC Drones
Make sure you file your Flight Requests
For recurrent or on-going activity, create a Project application but don’t forget to add flights before you head out.
After an incident, make sure you take pictures of the damage and file a post-flight report. Add as much detail as possible.

title: Training Programs
text:
Work in Progress
This page highlights potential training opportunities for UC Police Department (UCPD) UAS programs. Content is preliminary and intended to support discussion and prioritization.

title: Training Programs
text:
Training Topics Under Discussion

FAA regulatory training

Part 107 knowledge refreshers
Public aircraft vs civil operations
Waivers and special authorizations

Initial and recurrent flight training

Pilot onboarding and qualification
Proficiency and currency expectations
Emergency and abnormal procedures

Public safety mission training

Search and rescue support
Situational awareness and overwatch
Large events and perimeter operations

Advanced operations

Night operations
Operations over people (where applicable)
Multi‑agency coordination

Program management and governance

SOP development and maintenance
Documentation and recordkeeping
Equipment standardization considerations

Workshops and shared exercises

Scenario‑based training
Tabletop exercises
Systemwide or regional workshops


This list is not exhaustive and will evolve as priorities are refined.

title: Initial Website Completed
text:
The initial version of this website is now live.
At this stage, content is intentionally limited. The primary materials currently available focus on insurance coverage and shielded BVLOS operations applicable to University of California public safety UAS activity.

Current Content

Insurance information

UC UAS liability coverage
Physical damage coverage
Claim and documentation requirements
Coverage considerations for campus police operations

Shielded BVLOS Operations

UC Public Safety Organization Shielded Operations Waiver
Eligibility and operational scope
Required training and registration
Post‑flight documentation requirements


Additional content will be added incrementally as materials are developed and reviewed. This site is intended to serve as a practical reference point for public safety UAS operations across the UC system.
